originalAlarm.description: Value stored to '__eckey_814' is never read

sanitizedDescription: Value stored to 'eckey' is never read
model:
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  true)
(define-fun HAVE_ECC () Bool
  true)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __malloc_and_calloc_defined () Bool
  false)
reasoning: 35ae2e259e4.c L10: Dead store of `eckey` at `eckey = malloc(sizeof(int))`. However, eckey IS subsequently read via dereference at L12 (`*eckey = 30`) and L17 (`*eckey = 15`). Dereferencing eckey reads its pointer value, so the stored value at L10 is not dead. False positive from the CSA — possibly due to the library macro state (!_STDLIB_H AND __need_malloc_and_calloc) representing a non-standard partial stdlib.h include where malloc semantics are not fully modeled. PC includes restrictive library macros alongside HAVE_ECC=T.
